page: Amazon Web Services m8g.large is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz on the arm64 architecture. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and features a 36 MB L3 cache. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1707.09 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1811.0. Single-threaded compression tasks perform strongly, as demonstrated by a gzip compression speed of 30.28 GB/sec and a bzip3 decompression speed of 14.17 GB/sec. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance is weaker, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3402.97 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3229.0. Memory bandwidth is average for large uncached reads at 56.21 GB/sec, but weak for large uncached writes at 22.51 GB/sec.

The m8g.large offers a balanced resource profile for general-purpose workloads. Given its dedicated CPU allocation and 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it is qualitatively cost-efficient for entry-level applications that require consistent CPU performance without the overhead of shared-core configurations. It is suitable for lightweight database operations, text processing, and basic web serving, but is less optimal for multi-threaded compilation, ray tracing, or large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs on the arm64 architecture, powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, a 36 MB L3 cache, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal average single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-core execution and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. Qualitatively, the dedicated CPU allocation provides stable resource density for entry-level workloads. The m8g.large is best suited for general-purpose applications, lightweight database operations, text processing, and basic web serving.

Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and a 36 MB L3 cache
